title: Add --list-chapters to display epub chapter index
description:
Add a --list-chapters (-L conflicts with token limit, use a different short flag or none) flag that prints all chapters in the epub with their ordinal number and title, then exits. This lets users inspect what's available before running -c.

Example output:
  1. Cover
  2. Title Page
  3. Table of Contents
  4. Chapter 1: The Beginning
  5. Chapter 2: The Journey
  ...

Implementation: load the epub via the epub plugin, iterate the spine, print numbered list to stdout, exit 0.
notes: Implemented --list-chapters in epub plugin (flag) and cli.ts (handler). Prints numbered chapter list from spine and exits.

title: Support open-ended chapter ranges like 3-
description:
Allow -c to accept open-ended ranges such as '3-' meaning 'from chapter 3 to the end'. Currently only closed ranges (e.g. 3-8) and explicit lists (e.g. 3,4,5) are supported.

Examples:
- '-c 3-' → chapters 3 through the last chapter
- '-c -5' → chapters 1 through 5 (optional, less common)

The range parsing lives in the epub plugin's chapter argument handler.

title: Convert chapter HTML to plain text or markdown before summarizing
description:
Currently we feed raw HTML from the epub to the LLM. This wastes tokens on markup. Add a conversion step that strips HTML to clean text or markdown before sending to the LLM.

We already have html-rewriter-wasm installed. Use it (or an alternative) to:
1. Strip tags and produce clean plain text, or
2. Convert HTML to markdown (preserving headings, emphasis, etc.)

This should be a configurable option (--format text|markdown|html, defaulting to text or markdown) in the epub plugin. The conversion happens in epub/reader.ts before the HTML is passed to the summarize prompt.

title: Allow user-provided prompt instructions
description:
Add a --prompt flag (-q) that lets users provide additional instructions for the summarization prompt. This gets appended to the system prompt sent to the LLM. Examples:
- '--prompt "focus on character development and plot twists"'
- '--prompt "write in Spanish"'
- '--prompt "keep summaries under 100 words"'

The flag accepts a string that's appended after the standard chapter summarization instructions.
notes: Implemented --prompt/-q flag in opencode plugin. Appends user instructions after the standard chapter prompt, before the HTML content.

title: Resume existing opencode session across runs
description:
Always print the session ID to stderr after creating or resuming one. Add a --session flag (-s) that takes a session ID to resume instead of creating a new one. When resuming, assume the session already has context (no preseed) unless --preseed is explicitly passed. This enables stress-testing by running many chapters through a single session across multiple invocations, and also lets users inspect/continue sessions manually.

Session ID format: print to stderr as 'Session: <id>' after creation or on resume.
- Without --session: create new session, print ID
- With --session <id>: resume that session, skip preseed
- With --session <id> --preseed: resume that session, include existing summary in prompt

title: Graceful session rotation on context overflow
description:
opencode doesn't throw errors on context overflow — it auto-compacts the session by summarizing all messages into a condensed version. This manifests as:
1. A sudden drop in token count (2.1M → 38k observed)
2. A message with very high input tokens (149k) appearing as a compaction event
3. An empty user message or spontaneous 'continue' prompt from the system

Detection: after each chapter, check if getSessionUsage() returns a token count significantly LOWER than the previous chapter's. A drop of more than ~50% indicates compaction happened. When detected:
1. Log the compaction to stderr
2. Reset the session
3. Redo the current chapter in the fresh session (with preseed)
4. Continue

This replaces the error-based catch approach since no error is thrown.

title: Session rotation when approaching context limit
description:
Add a --context-limit flag that accepts either a percentage (e.g. '80%') or an absolute token count (e.g. '100000'). During multi-chapter processing, after each chapter summary, check cumulative token usage against the limit. If approaching the threshold, close the current session, start a new one, and seed it with the in-memory summary (seed summary already implemented). Transparent to the user — summaries continue flowing to the output file uninterrupted.

Depends on: ps-multi-chapter-session (needs the chapter loop and session reuse to be in place first).

title: Print context usage after each chapter summary
description:
After summarizing a chapter, fetch session messages via client.session.messages(), sum token counts from all AssistantMessages (input, output, reasoning, cache.read, cache.write), and print a summary line to stderr. Also show the selected model's context limit (from provider.list() model metadata) so the user can see how much headroom remains.

Format: something like:
  Tokens: 12,450 in / 890 out / 1,200 cache | Context: 12,450 / 1,000,000 (1.2%)

This is a building block for ps-multi-chapter-session — once we can track usage per chapter, we can auto-rotate sessions at a threshold.

title: Show connected providers and default models in listing
description: Use client.provider.list() to cross-reference connected providers with the default model map. --list-providers should only show connected providers (from the connected array) and mark which model is the default for each. --list-models should mark the default model for each connected provider. The default map from provider.list() covers all known providers; filter to only connected ones. This replaces the current approach of using config.providers() which lacks connection status.

title: Pass existing summary markdown into new session
description:
When starting a new session (either explicitly or because context limit was reached), pass the current summary markdown file content as context at the beginning of the session. This way the LLM has continuity with what's already been written — it knows the style, format, and prior chapter summaries even across session boundaries.

Works in conjunction with ps-markdown-output: if --output is specified, read the existing file and include it as a preamble in the first prompt of a new session.

title: Append summaries to markdown file instead of stdout
description:
Add a --output flag that specifies a markdown file to append summaries to. Preferably pass the filename to opencode as a resource/context so the LLM knows it's writing into an existing document and can format accordingly (headers, continuity with prior content).

If we can pass the file as a resource via the opencode SDK (file attachment or path context), do that. Otherwise, at minimum capture the LLM output and append it to the specified markdown file with a chapter heading.

title: Multi-chapter loop: iterate chapters in a single run
description:
Support processing multiple chapters in a single CLI invocation.

Building blocks now in place:
- Context usage tracking (ps-context-usage) — DONE
- Markdown file output with append (ps-markdown-output) — DONE
- Seed summary from existing file (ps-seed-summary) — DONE

What this ticket covers:
- Accept multiple chapters: -c 5-8 or -c 5,6,7 or repeated -c flags
- Loop through chapters in-process, accumulating the summary in memory
- Reuse a single opencode session across all chapters (send each as a new message)
- After each chapter, print context usage stats to stderr
- Append each chapter summary to the output file incrementally
- Does NOT handle session rotation — that is ps-session-rotation

title: Select provider and model via CLI flags
description: Support --provider and --model flags to choose which provider and model to use for the opencode session. Currently the model plugin registers these flags but selection should validate the choice exists in the provider list, show a friendly error if not found, and persist the selection for the session. Consider also supporting a default config so users don't have to pass flags every time.

title: List providers and models with current default indicator
description: When using --list-providers and --list-models, indicate which provider/model is the current default. The default comes from config.get() model field or from the provider list's default map. Highlight or mark the default row in the output table so the user knows what will be used if no --provider/--model is specified.

title: mDNS address finding for opencode service
description: Support discovering opencode service address via mDNS (multicast DNS). The plugin should browse for service records, pick a random one from the results, and use that as the baseUrl for the opencode SDK client. This replaces the hardcoded localhost:4096 address.
